# `liber:time@1`

Generated reference for the `liber:time` package (`time.lsidl`). See [the LSIDL language](../../../LSIDL.md).

LiberSystem time package - wall clock service.

## Imports

- `error` from [`liber:base@1`](/docs/gen/liber/base/v1.md)

## Types

### record `timestamp`

A wall-clock instant: seconds since the Unix epoch (1970-01-01 UTC). The canonical time object - the CLI renders it as ISO-8601 / epoch / human, all representations of these seconds.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `unix-secs` | `u64` |  |

## Interfaces

### interface `time`

TimeService: the wall clock. `now` returns the current UTC instant, computed as the monotonic clock plus an offset disciplined at boot from the RTC and an SNTP query. The offset is set only by TimeService's own RTC/NTP logic (it alone holds the network capability it needs) - there is no client-facing "set the clock" op, so no ambient authority can move wall time.

Request `[op u16][corr u32][args]`, reply `[corr u32][result]`.

| op | method | signature | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `now` | `now() -> result<timestamp, error>` |  |
